00024 #include "wrappers.h"
00025 
00026 #include "class.h"
00027 #include "memory.h"
00028 #include "util.h"
00029 #include "vm.h"
00030 
00031 /******************************************************************************
00032  * Debugging facilities                                                       *
00033  ******************************************************************************/
00034 
00035 #ifndef NDEBUG
00036 
00044 void dbg_warning_internal(const char *filename, int lineno,
00045                           const char *format, ... )
00046 {
00047     va_list args;
00048 
00049     fprintf(stderr, "%s:%d: WARNING: ", filename, lineno);
00050     va_start(args, format);
00051     vfprintf(stderr, format, args);
00052     va_end(args);
00053     fputc('\n', stderr);
00054 } // dbg_warning_internal()
00055 
00062 void dbg_error_internal(const char *filename, int lineno,
00063                         const char *format, ... )
00064 {
00065     va_list args;
00066 
00067     fprintf(stderr, "%s:%d: ERROR: ", filename, lineno);
00068     va_start(args, format);
00069     vfprintf(stderr, format, args);
00070     va_end(args);
00071     fputc('\n', stderr);
00072 } // dbg_error_internal()
00073 
00080 void dbg_printf_internal(const char *filename, int lineno,
00081                          const char *format, ... )
00082 {
00083     va_list args;
00084 
00085     fprintf(stderr, "%s:%d: ", filename, lineno);
00086     va_start(args, format);
00087     vfprintf(stderr, format, args);
00088     va_end(args);
00089     fputc('\n', stderr);
00090 } // dbg_printf_internal()
00091 
00097 void dbg_unreachable_internal(const char *filename, int lineno)
00098 {
00099     fprintf(stderr, "%s:%d: ERROR: Impossible control flow\n", filename,
00100             lineno);
00101     abort();
00102 } // dbg_unreachable_internal()
00103 
00104 #endif // !NDEBUG
00105 
00106 /******************************************************************************
00107  * String functions                                                           *
00108  ******************************************************************************/
00109 
00116 char *cstrchrnul(const char *s, int c)
00117 {
00118     while ((*s != '\0') && (*s != c)) {
00119         s++;
00120     }
00121 
00122     return (char *) s;
00123 } // cstrchrnul()
00124 
00125 /******************************************************************************
00126  * Utility functions                                                          *
00127  ******************************************************************************/
00128 
00135 void sort_asc_uint16_ptrs(uint16_t *array, void **ptrs, uint32_t len)
00136 {
00137     uint32_t i = 0;
00138     uint32_t k = len - 1;
00139     uint32_t min, max, j, temp;
00140     void *temp_ptr;
00141 
00142     while (i < k) {
00143         min = i;
00144         max = i;
00145 
00146         for (j = i + 1; j <= k; j++) {
00147             if (array[j] < array[min]) {
00148                 min = j;
00149             }
00150 
00151             if (array[j] > array[max]) {
00152                 max = j;
00153             }
00154         }
00155 
00156         temp = array[min];
00157         temp_ptr = ptrs[min];
00158         array[min] = array[i];
00159         ptrs[min] = ptrs[i];
00160         array[i] = temp;
00161         ptrs[i] = temp_ptr;
00162 
00163         if (max == i) {
00164             temp = array[min];
00165             temp_ptr = ptrs[min];
00166             array[min] = array[k];
00167             ptrs[min] = ptrs[k];
00168             array[k] = temp;
00169             ptrs[k] = temp_ptr;
00170         } else {
00171             temp = array[max];
00172             temp_ptr = ptrs[max];
00173             array[max] = array[k];
00174             ptrs[max] = ptrs[k];
00175             array[k] = temp;
00176             ptrs[k] = temp_ptr;
00177         }
00178 
00179         i++;
00180         k--;
00181     }
00182 } // sort_asc_uint16_ptrs()
00183 
00184 /******************************************************************************
00185  * Exceptions                                                                 *
00186  ******************************************************************************/
00187 
00188 #if !NDEBUG
00189 
00197 void c_throw_dbg(const char *file, int line, int exc, const char *desc, ...)
00198 {
00199     thread_t *self = thread_self();
00200     va_list args, copy;
00201     char dummy[1] = { 0 };
00202     size_t len;
00203 
00204     // Set the exception information
00205     self->c_exception.file = file;
00206     self->c_exception.line = line;
00207 
00208     if (desc != NULL) {
00209         // Calculate the length of the exception message ...
00210         va_start(args, desc);
00211         va_copy(copy, args);
00212         len = vsnprintf(dummy, 1, desc, copy);
00213         va_end(copy);
00214 
00215         // ... and print it into the exception description
00216         self->c_exception.description = gc_malloc(len + 1);
00217         vsnprintf(self->c_exception.description, len + 1, desc, args);
00218         va_end(args);
00219     } else {
00220         desc = NULL;
00221     }
00222 
00223     longjmp(*(self->c_exception.buf), exc); // Jump to the closest catcher
00224 } // c_throw_dbg()
00225 
00226 #else
00227 
00233 void c_throw(int exc, const char *desc, ...)
00234 {
00235     thread_t *self = thread_self();
00236     va_list args, copy;
00237     char dummy[1] = { 0 };
00238     size_t len;
00239 
00240     if (desc != NULL) {
00241         // Calculate the length of the exception message ...
00242         va_start(args, desc);
00243         va_copy(copy, args);
00244         len = vsnprintf(dummy, 1, desc, copy);
00245         va_end(copy);
00246 
00247         // ... and print it into the exception description
00248         self->c_exception.description = gc_malloc(len + 1);
00249         vsnprintf(self->c_exception.description, len + 1, desc, args);
00250         va_end(args);
00251     } else {
00252         desc = NULL;
00253     }
00254 
00255     longjmp(*(self->c_exception.buf), exc); // Jump to the closest catcher
00256 } // c_throw()
00257 
00258 #endif // !NDEBUG
00259 
00262 void c_clear_exception( void )
00263 {
00264     thread_t *self = thread_self();
00265 
00266     gc_free(self->c_exception.description);
00267     self->c_exception.description = NULL;
00268 } // c_clear_exception()
00269 
00273 void c_print_exception(int exc)
00274 {
00275     static const char *names[] = {
00276         "",
00277         "java.lang.VirtualMachineError",
00278         "java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError"
00279     };
00280 
00281     thread_t *self = thread_self();
00282     const char *desc = self->c_exception.description;
00283 
00284 #ifndef NDEBUG
00285     printf("Thrown exception:\n"
00286            "   type:\t\t%s\n"
00287            "   description:\t\t%s\n"
00288            "   file:\t\t%s\n"
00289            "   line:\t\t%d\n",
00290            names[exc], desc ? desc : "", self->c_exception.file,
00291            self->c_exception.line);
00292 #else
00293     printf("Thrown exception:\n"
00294            "    type:\t\t%s\n"
00295            "    description:\t\t%s\n",
00296            names[exc], desc ? desc : "");
00297 #endif // !NDEBUG
00298 } // c_print_exception()
00299 
00300 /******************************************************************************
00301  * Time functionality                                                         *
00302  ******************************************************************************/
00303 
00310 struct timespec get_time_with_offset(uint64_t ms, uint32_t nanos)
00311 {
00312     struct timespec res;
00313 
00314 #if HAVE_CLOCK_GETTIME
00315     clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &res);
00316 #elif HAVE_GETTIMEOFDAY
00317     struct timeval now;
00318 
00319     gettimeofday(&now, NULL);
00320     res.tv_sec = now.tv_sec;
00321     res.tv_nsec = now.tv_usec * 1000;
00322 #else
00323 #   error "get_time_with_offset() doesn't have an appropriate fallback."
00324 #endif
00325 
00326     res.tv_sec += (ms / 1000);
00327     res.tv_nsec += (ms % 1000) * 1000000 + nanos;
00328 
00329     if (res.tv_nsec > 1000000000) {
00330         res.tv_nsec -= 1000000000;
00331         res.tv_sec++;
00332     }
00333 
00334     return res;
00335 } // get_time_with_offset()
00336
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:38:46 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
